title: Review VISU_DATA Banner Messages And Current Login Status
summary: Use the VISU_DATA screen on the operator station HMI to review alarm or informational banner messages, navigate through available messages with the banner arrows, and verify which user ID is currently logged in.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to check alarm or informational messages shown on the VISU_DATA screen and confirm the current logged-in user ID at the operator station.
not_for: Correcting banner message conditions or faults, because no corrective action is provided in the source.
Changing login state or user permissions beyond viewing the displayed current user ID.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Navigate to the VISU_DATA screen, review banner messages, and verify the displayed logged-in user ID.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to viewing screen information on the operator station HMI.
No corrective action, reset action, or maintenance action is provided by the source for this procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

system_preconditions: VISU_DATA screen is available on the operator station HMI
external_references: Figure 4-20 Operator Station HMI Data Screen

visual_references.description: Operator station display controls showing screen-navigation buttons and mapping including F5 to Visu_Data.
VISU_DATA screen layout showing login controls, informational banner, navigation arrows, and logged-in user ID display.
Additional VISU_DATA screen reference from the same section.

steps.title: Navigate to the VISU_DATA screen
steps.purpose: Access the HMI screen that contains the informational banner and current login display.
steps.instruction: Open or navigate to the VISU_DATA screen using the operator station HMI screen selection controls.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_DATA screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can access the VISU_DATA screen and view its layout.
steps.failure_condition: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be reached using the available screen selection controls.

actions.label: Open VISU_DATA
actions.exact_action: Open or navigate to the VISU_DATA screen using the operator station HMI screen selection controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen-navigation controls and the mapping that includes F5 to Visu_Data.
Overall VISU_DATA layout and screen-selection area.

stop_or_escalate_if: VISU_DATA cannot be accessed from the operator station HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Next Screen drop-down menu, the buttons on the right side of the screen, and the physical buttons on the right side of the display navigate to the same screens. The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."
Figure 4-20 shows the Operator Station HMI Data Screen.

steps.title: Locate the informational banner
steps.purpose: Identify the area of the VISU_DATA screen where alarms or informational messages are displayed.
steps.instruction: Locate the informational banner on the VISU_DATA screen that displays alarms or informational messages.
steps.expected_result: The informational banner is identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: A banner area for alarms or informational messages is visible.
steps.failure_condition: The informational banner cannot be identified on the VISU_DATA screen.

actions.label: Find message banner
actions.exact_action: Locate the informational banner that displays alarms or informational messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Informational banner area on the VISU_DATA screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The informational banner is not visible where expected on the VISU_DATA screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Navigate through banner messages
steps.purpose: Review all available messages shown in the informational banner.
steps.instruction: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through available messages.
steps.expected_result: The displayed banner message changes as the operator navigates through messages.
steps.healthy_condition: Banner arrows allow movement through available messages.
steps.failure_condition: Banner messages cannot be navigated using the arrows on the right side of the banner.

actions.label: Use banner arrows
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Message banner and the navigation arrows on the right side of the banner.

stop_or_escalate_if: Banner messages cannot be navigated using the documented arrows.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages.

steps.title: Read displayed banner messages
steps.purpose: Observe the content of each alarm or informational message presented in the banner.
steps.instruction: Read each displayed alarm or informational message from the banner as you navigate through the available messages.
steps.expected_result: The operator has reviewed the available alarm or informational messages shown in the banner.
steps.healthy_condition: Messages are visible and readable in the banner.
steps.failure_condition: Messages are not readable or cannot be fully reviewed.

actions.label: Review banner text
actions.exact_action: Read each displayed alarm or informational message from the banner.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Login controls, message banner, and surrounding VISU_DATA screen elements while reviewing messages.

stop_or_escalate_if: Displayed messages are unreadable or cannot be reviewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Locate the current logged-in user field
steps.purpose: Find the screen field that identifies the current operator session.
steps.instruction: Locate the field on the VISU_DATA screen that displays the ID of the user currently logged in.
steps.expected_result: The current logged-in user ID field is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: A field showing the currently logged-in user ID is visible on the screen.
steps.failure_condition: The current logged-in user ID field cannot be located.

actions.label: Find logged-in user field
actions.exact_action: Locate the field that displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed logged-in user ID field near the login controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed current user ID field is missing or cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

steps.title: Verify the displayed login status
steps.purpose: Confirm whether a user is currently shown as logged in and capture the displayed user ID if needed.
steps.instruction: Verify whether a user ID is shown and record the displayed user ID if needed.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms whether a user ID is displayed and can identify the current logged-in user.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed login state appears consistent with the actual operator session.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed login state or user ID appears inconsistent with the actual operator session.

actions.label: Verify login status
actions.exact_action: Verify whether a user ID is shown and record the displayed user ID if needed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Current logged-in user ID display and nearby LOGIN/LOGOUT controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed login state or user ID appears inconsistent with the actual operator session.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

success_criteria: The VISU_DATA screen is accessed successfully.
Available alarm or informational banner messages can be viewed and navigated.
The currently logged-in user ID can be identified from the screen.
healthy_conditions: The informational banner is visible on the VISU_DATA screen.
The arrows on the right side of the banner navigate through messages.
The current logged-in user ID is displayed and appears consistent with the actual operator session.
failure_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be accessed.
The informational banner cannot be located or reviewed.
Banner messages cannot be navigated using the documented arrows.
The displayed login state or user ID appears inconsistent with the actual operator session.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if banner messages cannot be navigated using the documented arrows.
Escalate if the displayed login state or user ID appears inconsistent with the actual operator session.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station screen-navigation controls used to access VISU_DATA.
VISU_DATA screen layout including informational banner, navigation arrows, and current logged-in user ID.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The informational banner displays alarms or informational messages, the arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages, and the screen displays the ID of the user currently logged in.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source does not provide corrective actions for banner messages or login-state discrepancies.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required; this procedure appears observational only.
The source does not define how or where to record the displayed user ID.
